title: Philippines Formally Accuses China of Cyanide Sabotage at Second Thomas Shoal Garrison
detail: The Philippines on April 13, 2026 formally accused Chinese fishing vessels of deploying cyanide near Second Thomas Shoal (Ayungin Shoal), characterizing the incidents as deliberate 'sabotage' of the Philippine Marine garrison aboard BRP Sierra Madre. Laboratory tests confirmed the presence of toxic cyanide in plastic bottles seized by Philippine Navy personnel from Chinese boats during at least four separate incidents spanning February 2025 to March 2026 — yellow dishwashing-liquid bottles commonly repurposed as cyanide containers in the South China Sea. NSC spokesperson Jonathan Malaya warned the tactic was designed to kill fish populations around the reef, depriving the ~12-member garrison of a critical supplemental food source, while potentially degrading the structural integrity of the reef platform supporting the grounded warship. Cyanide fishing destroys coral ecosystems and eliminates fish populations within targeted areas. China's Foreign Ministry dismissed the allegations as a 'stunt,' claiming Chinese boats were engaged in lawful fishing and alleging the Philippines fabricated evidence. The accusation marks an escalation in the category of alleged Chinese gray-zone tactics, extending beyond water cannons, lasers, and bladed weapons to alleged chemical sabotage of reef ecology.

media.caption: Philippines accuses China of cyanide sabotage at BRP Sierra Madre garrison, Second Thomas Shoal

title: PLA Southern Theater Calls April SCS Patrols 'Routine,' Condemns Philippines for 'Rallying Extra-Regional Countries'
detail: China's PLA Southern Theater Command released a statement on April 13, 2026 characterizing its South China Sea naval patrols as 'routine' and criticizing the Philippines for 'rallying extra-regional countries' to conduct joint maritime exercises that Beijing said 'stir up troubles and undermine regional peace and stability.' The statement followed the April 9-12 trilateral US-Australia-Philippines Maritime Cooperative Activity (MCA) conducted within the Philippine Exclusive Economic Zone, which included warships, fighter jets, and surveillance aircraft from all three nations. Southern Theater Command stated PLA forces had conducted 'tracking, monitoring, warning off, and expulsion' of vessels in waters it claims — language identical to the March 30 statement after the PLA Combat Readiness Patrol near Scarborough Shoal. Beijing's parallel narrative frames each allied exercise as 'destabilizing' while characterizing PLA patrol responses as lawful law enforcement. The timing of the statement — coinciding with the Philippines' cyanide accusation — reflects coordinated state-media management of competing narratives about South China Sea events.

media.caption: PLA Southern Theater Command issues statement on South China Sea patrols after Philippines-US-Australia drills
